title: Creamy Chickpea & Chicken Fattet
summary: Creamy, tangy, crunchy and nutty. This fun and warming Middle Eastern dish is all about texture.

method.description: Drain the chickpeas, reserving the liquid. Thinly slice the garlic. Pick the parsley leaves. finely zest the lemon, then cut it in half and juice it.

Add the chicken breasts, 7-spice and garlic to a large saucepan and pour over the stock. Season with salt and pepper. Set over a medium heat and bring to the boil, then simmer gently for 10–15 mins until the chicken is cooked through.
Meanwhile, chop the flatbread into 2cm bite-sized pieces. Tip them onto a baking tray, sprinkle over the almonds and drizzle with olive oil. Slide into the oven for 5–8 mins until golden and crisp.
In a bowl, stir the tahini and Greek yoghurt together. Season with salt and a squeeze of lemon juice.

Once the chicken is cooked, transfer it to a board and shred using two forks. Return the shredded chicken to the pan with the cooking liquid. Turn the heat up, add the chickpeas and simmer until the liquid is mostly evaporated. Take off the heat and leave to cool for a couple of minutes, then stir through the tahini yoghurt until creamy. Add a splash of chickpea liquid if you want it saucier. Taste and season again if needed.

Spoon the creamy chicken mixture into bowls. Top with the crispy flatbread pieces, toasted almonds and parsley leaves. Finish with a sprinkle of lemon zest, some freshly cracked black pepper and any remaining lemon wedges on the side.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
